info.name: Maugood v1.0 — Local Testing
info.description:
Comprehensive API collection for testing Maugood locally. Organized by role and capability.

## Setup

1. Import this collection into Postman.
2. Import the matching environment file (`Maugood-Local.postman_environment.json`).
3. Open `credentials.txt` from your repo root.
4. Edit the environment variables and paste the actual passwords from credentials.txt:
   - `super_admin_password`
   - `admin_mts_demo_password`
   - `hr_mts_demo_password`
   - `manager_eng_mts_demo_password`
   - `dual_role_mts_demo_password`
   - `employee_mts_demo_password`
   - `admin_inaisys_password`
   - `employee_inaisys_password`
5. Make sure your Maugood backend is running at `http://localhost:8000`.
6. Run requests in order. Login requests automatically capture the session cookie into a Postman cookie jar; subsequent requests use it.

## How to use

- Each folder = a role or capability area.
- Login folders set the active session. Switch sessions by re-running the relevant Login request.
- Tests are attached to most requests — they auto-extract IDs (e.g. `tenant_id`, `employee_id`, `request_id`) into environment variables so later requests can chain.
- Collection runner can execute each folder end-to-end as a regression check.

## Conventions

- All requests use `{{base_url}}` so you can swap localhost for a deployed instance.
- Tenant slug is sent in the login body (`tenant_slug` field). Cookie carries it after login.
- 401 from any non-login request means the session expired or the wrong role is active — re-run the relevant login.

item.name: 0. Health & Diagnostics
item.description: Run these first to confirm the backend is alive and identify which endpoints exist.

exec: pm.test('Returns 401 when not logged in', () => pm.response.to.have.status(401));

item.name: 7. Inaisys (real corporate, real camera)
